task_completion.description: Verify the global agent label is configured with the correct key and value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Wazuh manager configuration file (typically ossec.conf) being edited or viewed. It should contain a <labels> section with <label key="compliance">pci_dss</label>.

task_completion.description: Verify the Wazuh manager is configured to monitor the specified application log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a <localfile> block in the configuration file containing <location>/var/log/payment_app.log</location> and <log_format>syslog</log_format>.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom detection rule is created with the correct ID and alert level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for local_rules.xml being edited or viewed. It should contain a new rule definition starting with <rule id="100250" level="12">.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule triggers on the correct phrase.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look within the rule 100250 definition in local_rules.xml for the match condition: <match>Transaction: VOID</match>.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule only triggers if the agent has the 'compliance' label set to 'pci_dss'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look within the rule 100250 definition in local_rules.xml for a field condition checking the label, such as <field name="labels.compliance">pci_dss</field> or similar Wazuh syntax.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually edited the configuration files rather than just printing text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor interface (like nano, vim) or proper file modification commands (like sed, or echo appending to a file) targeting the actual Wazuh configuration paths (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf and /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent is working within the correct Wazuh manager container.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal prompts or docker commands indicating the agent is executing commands inside the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' container.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent didn't just print a fake success message or output the expected XML to the console without saving.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show standard command execution, file editing, and saving processes, not just an 'echo' of the final expected XML block to the standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Wazuh manager configuration must include a label with key 'compliance' and value 'pci_dss'. The monitored log file must be '/var/log/payment_app.log' with format 'syslog'. The custom rule in local_rules.xml must have ID '100250', level '12', match the string 'Transaction: VOID', and include a condition to check for the 'compliance' label value 'pci_dss'. The target container is 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1'.
